docs.title: gh-mkpage
docs.text: Welcome to gh-mkpage — a project for generating and deploying markdown documentation pages from GitHub.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
• Docs engine: MkDocs with Material theme
  • Diagrams: Mermaid.js via pymdownx.superfences
  • Deploy: GitHub Actions → gh-pages branch
  • PR previews: Per-PR subfolders, auto-cleaned on merge

docs.title: Explore
docs.text:
• Contributing — local dev, adding skills, CI/CD pipeline
  • Skills — 16 agent skills with demo guides
  • Architecture — site config, custom JS, git hooks
  • Projects — all mibracy GitHub repos
  • Updates — project progress logs with semver

Braun dark mode default. Customise via the style picker icon in the header.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: How the pieces of gh-mkpage fit together — from config to custom JS to deployment.

docs.title: Layers
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[mkdocs.yml] --> B[MkDocs build]
  C[docs/ *.md] --> B
  D[extra_css] --> B
  E[extra_js] --> B
  B --> F[Static HTML site]
  F --> G[gh-pages branch]
  G --> H[GitHub Pages]

docs.title: Component guide
docs.text: Component Location Purpose Site config mkdocs.yml Theme, nav, extensions, plugins Custom CSS docs/stylesheets/extra.css Style picker modal, light sepia background Custom JS docs/javascripts/prefs.js Style picker logic, palette persistence Custom JS docs/javascripts/mermaid-zoom.js Mermaid diagram zoom + pan Content docs/ All markdown pages Skills .agents/skills/ Agent skill instructions Hooks .githooks/ Pre-commit demo enforcement CI/CD .github/workflows/ Build, deploy, CodeQL

docs.title: Theme combos
docs.text:
Open the style picker (paintbrush icon in the header) to try these:

Primary Accent Scheme Vibe brown amber slate Default — earthy dark teal cyan slate Deep tech dark deep-purple amber slate Royal dark blue-grey light-blue slate Muted professional brown amber default Default — warm light (sepia bg) blue orange default Clean light with pop green lime default Fresh light red orange default Warm light

docs.title: Style picker (prefs.js)
docs.text: Provides a modal for choosing primary and accent colors. Persists to localStorage.__style_picker.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. On DOMContentLoaded and DOMContentSwitch, restores saved colors by setting data-md-color-primary and data-md-color-accent on <body>.
  2. Injects a palette button into the header (before the dark/light toggle).
  3. Opens a modal with color swatches on click.
  4. Reset clears saved prefs and reloads, returning to Material defaults.

docs.title: Persistence
docs.text: Uses localStorage with key __style_picker (stringified JSON {primary, accent}).

docs.title: Mermaid zoom (mermaid-zoom.js)
docs.text: Adds pan and zoom to all Mermaid SVG diagrams.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. A MutationObserver watches .md-content for new svg elements that are children of .mermaid.
  2. On DOMContentSwitch (instant navigation), scans for any Mermaid SVGs already present.
  3. Wraps each SVG in a transparent overlay and attaches wheel (zoom) and mousedown/mousemove/mouseup (pan) handlers.
  4. Zoom centers on cursor position using CSS transform: scale() + translate().

docs.title: Page transitions
docs.text: On DOMContentSwitch, the .md-content element gets a CSS animation (fade in + translateY) via @keyframes pageEnter. The animation is reset on each navigation via requestAnimationFrame.

docs.title: Pre-commit hook
docs.text:
Location: .githooks/pre-commit

Purpose: Enforce that every new skill under .agents/skills/<name>/ has a matching demo file at docs/skills/<name>-demo.md.

How it works:

  1. Finds all directories under .agents/skills/
  2. For each, checks that docs/skills/<name>-demo.md exists
  3. If any are missing, prints the list and exits non-zero

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
git config core.hooksPath .githooks

The hook only checks files that are staged in the current commit, so existing skills without demos won't block unrelated changes.

docs.title: Theme
docs.text:
Uses squidfunk/mkdocs-material with the following features:

  • navigation.instant — SPA-like fetch + DOM swap for faster navigation
  • navigation.instant.progress — progress bar during page loads
  • navigation.indexes — uses folder index.md as section overview
  • navigation.tracking — URL hash updates on scroll

docs.title: Palette
docs.text:
The first palette entry is the default (dark mode). Two schemes:

Scheme Default Colors slate Yes primary: brown, accent: amber default No primary: brown, accent: amber

The toggle icon swaps between sun and moon.

docs.title: Markdown extensions
docs.text: Only pymdownx.superfences with Mermaid custom fences is configured directly in mkdocs.yml. The full extension set in the mkdocs-material skill (SKILL.md) is available for reference but not enabled — add extensions as needed.

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text:
Plugin Purpose search Full-text client-side search

More plugins (blog, social, offline) can be added; see the mkdocs-material skill for config.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thanks for contributing to gh-mkpage! This guide covers local development, adding skills, and how the CI/CD pipeline works.

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text: Section Description Local development Preview the site, lint locally Adding skills Install new agent skills with demo files CI/CD pipeline How builds, deploys, and PR previews work

docs.title: Adding skills
docs.text: Agent skills live under .agents/skills/<name>/ with a SKILL.md entrypoint.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Create the skill directory

    mkdir -p .agents/skills/<name>/references

  2. Write SKILL.md — the skill instructions for the agent. Include progressive disclosure, code snippets, reference links.

  3. Create a demo file at docs/skills/<name>-demo.md

    The pre-commit hook enforces this, so it must exist before committing. Follow existing demo patterns: brief description, capabilities list, ROI to human.

  4. Register in the nav — add an entry under Skills in mkdocs.yml

  5. Register in AGENTS.md — add a bullet to the Installed skills section

  6. Update the skills index — add a row to the table in docs/skills/index.md

  7. Commit and push

    git add .agents/skills/<name>/ docs/skills/<name>-demo.md
git commit -m "Add <name> skill"

docs.title: Demo file template
docs.text:
# <Name> Demo

Brief description.

**Capabilities:**
1. Thing it can do
2. Thing it can do
3. Thing it can do

## ROI to human

Why this skill matters.

docs.title: CI/CD pipeline
docs.text: The docs site is built and deployed via GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Deploy docs (docs.yml)
docs.text:
Trigger Action Push to main Builds site with squidfunk/mkdocs-material, deploys to root of gh-pages PR open / update Builds site, deploys to preview/pr-# on gh-pages PR merged / closed Removes preview/pr-# folder from gh-pages

PR previews use keep_files: true so they never overwrite the root site or other previews.

docs.title: CodeQL (codeql.yml)
docs.text: Runs on push and PR to main, plus weekly. Scans JavaScript/TypeScript for security vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Local build
docs.text: docker run --rm -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material build

docs.title: Adding a workflow
docs.text:
1. Create .github/workflows/<name>.yml
  2. Test via a PR to main — the runner picks up new workflow files automatically

docs.title: Dependabot
docs.text: Configured in .github/dependabot.yml for github-actions and docker. Runs weekly and opens PRs for version bumps.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker (for mkdocs-material image)
  • Git with hooks installed

docs.title: Preview the site
docs.text:
docker run --rm -it -p 8000:8000 -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material

Opens at http://localhost:8000. Live-reloads on file changes.

docs.title: Install git hooks
docs.text:
git config core.hooksPath .githooks

The pre-commit hook enforces that every new skill under .agents/skills/<name>/ has a matching demo file at docs/skills/<name>-demo.md.

docs.title: Build without serving
docs.text:
docker run --rm -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material build

Output lands in site/ (gitignored).

docs.text: Repo Language Description gh-mkpage Shell Generate markdown documentation pages from GitHub megalinter-demo Java Demo project showcasing MegaLinter setup, fixes, and CI workflows notifai Go Agent Notify: AI-powered event notification system spring-autodocs Java Spring Boot REST API with auto-generated docs from code annotations showcase Python Dynamic video presentation generator with Remotion + WebSocket goat HTML Ticketing system backend with clean API and adaptable data model vibeshift JavaScript Transform vague "vibe" descriptions into structured AI prompts took Rust (no description) simple-kafka Java Kafka, H2, JSON/XML/PDF file generation example mibracy.github.io HTML Service to help generate JSON for devsecops pipeline poop-pwa TypeScript PWA for caregivers tracking hepatic encephalopathy patients cfc-discord-bot JavaScript A little quirky Discord bot piEnabler — Not just another PowerPoint clone PIE Dart 425 Senior Project BadassBattleship Java The most bad ass battleship program CS 325 has ever created

docs.title: Skills
docs.text: Skill Demo Caveman demo Create-Readme demo Diagnose demo Grill-Me demo Grill-With-Docs demo Handoff demo Mermaid Diagrams demo MkDocs Material demo Improve Architecture demo Prototype demo Setup Matt Pocock Skills demo TDD demo To-Issues demo To-PRD demo Triage demo Write-A-Skill demo Zoom-Out demo

docs.title: Caveman Demo
docs.text:
Caveman mode cuts token usage ~75%. Levels:

  • lite — no filler, keep full sentences
  • full — drop articles, fragments OK
  • ultra — abbreviate prose, arrows for causality

Example: Why component re-render? - lite: "Your component re-renders because you create a new object reference each render." - full: "New object ref each render. Wrap in useMemo." - ultra: "Inline obj prop → new ref → re-render. useMemo."

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Less tokens = faster sessions + lower cost. More signal, less fluff per turn.

docs.title: Create-Readme Demo
docs.text:
Generates a concise, well-structured README.md from project review. Uses senior engineer tone, GFM formatting, GitHub admonitions.

Usage: install skill, then tell agent "create a readme"

Sources inspiration from Azure Samples and other polished open-source readmes.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Professional README in seconds. No blank‑repo shame. Sets tone for contributors.

docs.title: Diagnose Demo
docs.text:
Debug loop: Reproduce → Minimise → Hypothesise → Instrument → Fix → Regression-test

For hard bugs and performance regressions.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Structured debug loop beats random printf. Less time stuck = faster fixes.

docs.title: Grill-Me Demo
docs.text:
Stress-test a plan or design through relentless questioning. Agent walks down each branch of the decision tree, resolving dependencies one-by-one.

Usage: "grill me on this plan" or "stress-test my design"

Process: agent asks questions one at a time, recommends answers, explores codebase when possible instead of asking.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Catches blind spots before you code. Saves rewrite cycles from bad assumptions.

docs.title: Grill-With-Docs Demo
docs.text:
Same as grill-me, but also challenges plans against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates CONTEXT.md + ADRs inline as decisions crystallise.

Usage: "grill this plan against our docs"

Key difference: reads existing domain docs, validates terminology, writes decisions to ADRs as you go.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Plan stays consistent with existing architecture. Docs evolve while you design, not after.

docs.title: Handoff Demo
docs.text:
Generates a compact handoff document for another agent. Use when context-switching or handing off long-running work.

Output: structured markdown with decisions, open questions, and next steps.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: No context loss between sessions or agents. Pick up exactly where you left off.

docs.title: Improve Codebase Architecture Demo
docs.text:
Surface architectural friction and propose deepening opportunities — refactors that turn shallow modules into deep ones.

Key concepts: - Module — anything with an interface + implementation - Depth — leverage at the interface (much behaviour behind small API) - Seam — where behaviour can be altered without editing in place - Adapter — concrete thing satisfying an interface at a seam

Informed by domain language in CONTEXT.md and decisions in docs/adr/.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Smaller interfaces, less coupling, easier testing. Pays off every future change.

docs.title: Mermaid Diagrams Demo
docs.text: Create professional software diagrams from text. Zoom/pan with mouse wheel + drag.

docs.title: Flowchart — CI/CD pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart TD
  A[Push to main] --> B{Build site}
  B --> C[Site dir]
  C --> D{Deploy target}
  D -->|main branch| E[gh-pages root]
  D -->|PR| F[gh-pages/preview/pr-N]
  E --> G[github.io]
  F --> G
  H[PR closed] --> I[Cleanup preview/pr-N]

docs.title: Sequence — API auth flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as Client
  participant G as Gateway
  participant A as Auth Service
  participant D as Database
  C->>G: GET /api/resource
  G->>A: Validate token
  A->>D: Check session
  D-->>A: Valid
  A-->>G: OK
  G->>G: Rate limit check
  G-->>C: 200 + data

docs.title: Class — Domain model
docs.text:
classDiagram
  class User {
    +UUID id
    +String name
    +String email
    +login() Token
    +logout() void
  }
  class Project {
    +UUID id
    +String slug
    +String repoUrl
    +deploy() void
  }
  class Deployment {
    +UUID id
    +String branch
    +String status
    +rollback() void
  }
  User "1" --> "*" Project : owns
  Project "1" --> "*" Deployment : has

docs.title: Entity Relationship — Schema
docs.text:
erDiagram
  USER ||--o{ PROJECT : owns
  PROJECT ||--|{ DEPLOYMENT : has
  PROJECT ||--o{ SKILL : configures
  USER {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string email
  }
  PROJECT {
    uuid id PK
    string slug
    string repo_url
  }
  DEPLOYMENT {
    uuid id PK
    string branch
    string status
    datetime created_at
  }

docs.title: State — Deployment lifecycle
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Queued
  Queued --> Building
  Building --> Deploying
  Deploying --> Live
  Deploying --> Failed
  Live --> RollingBack
  RollingBack --> Live
  Failed --> Queued
  Live --> [*]

docs.text:
gitGraph

  commit id: "release"

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Diagrams that live in version control, stay current with code, and render anywhere.

docs.title: MkDocs-Material Demo
docs.text:
Expert guidance for building documentation sites with Material for MkDocs. Covers configuration, content patterns, theme customization, and deployment.

Capabilities: 1. Full mkdocs.yml scaffolding with theme, nav, palette, extensions 2. Navigation pattern selection (tabs, sections, indexes, instant) 3. Admonition, tabs, annotation, Mermaid diagram content patterns 4. Plugin setup (search, social, blog, offline, privacy) 5. Troubleshooting common issues (Mermaid rendering, palette toggles, mobile nav)

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Accelerate docs iteration and eliminate manual config lookup. Treats mkdocs-material as a first-class platform with turnkey snippets.

docs.title: Prototype Demo
docs.text:
Build throwaway code to answer a question. Two branches:

  • Logic — interactive terminal app to push a state machine through edge cases
  • UI — multiple radically different UI variations switchable via URL param + bottom bar

Usage: "prototype this data model", "try a few designs for this page"

Tip: pick the branch based on the question being answered, not the format preference.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Answers design questions before committing to code. Fail fast on paper, not in prod.

docs.title: Setup-Matt-Pocock-Skills Demo
docs.text:
One-time setup skill. Must run before using to-issues, to-prd, triage, diagnose, tdd, improve-codebase-architecture, or zoom-out.

What it configures: - Issue tracker (GitHub, Linear, or local markdown) - Triage label vocabulary (what labels to use for each triage role) - Domain doc layout (where CONTEXT.md and ADRs live)

Usage: load the skill, let the agent explore, answer its questions.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Run once, unlocks 7 downstream skills. Essential wiring for the whole skill suite.

docs.title: TDD Demo
docs.text:
Red-green-refactor loop:

  1. Red — write failing test
  2. Green — write minimal code to pass
  3. Refactor — clean up while keeping tests green

Run focused tests: pnpm vitest run -- [test-pattern]

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Red‑green‑refactor: always know what works. Fewer regressions, safer refactors.

docs.title: To-Issues Demo
docs.text:
Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues using vertical slices (tracer bullets).

Prerequisite: run /setup-matt-pocock-skills first.

Process: gather context → explore codebase → break into vertical-slice issues → publish to tracker with labels.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Big plans become grab‑and‑go tickets. Clear scope, clear ownership, less overhead.

docs.title: To-PRD Demo
docs.text:
Turn current conversation context into a published PRD. Does NOT interview the user — synthesises from what's already discussed.

Prerequisite: run /setup-matt-pocock-skills first.

Output: structured PRD posted to the issue tracker with ready-for-agent label.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Conversation → spec in one shot. No template fiddling, no formatting busywork.

docs.title: Triage Demo
docs.text:
Move issues through a state machine of triage roles. Every AI comment during triage starts with:

This was generated by AI during triage.

Includes reference docs for writing durable agent briefs and a .out-of-scope/ knowledge base convention.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Stops issue pile‑up. Clear state machine prevents dropped tickets and repeated work.

docs.title: Write-A-Skill Demo
docs.text:
Create new agent skills with proper structure and progressive disclosure.

Process: 1. Gather requirements (task, use cases, scripts needed, reference materials) 2. Draft SKILL.md + reference files + utility scripts 3. Install and test

Follows the skills.sh conventions for packaging agent skills.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Package repeat workflows as reusable skills. Stop teaching agents the same thing twice.

docs.title: Zoom-Out Demo
docs.text:
Asks the agent to go up a layer of abstraction and provide broader context.

Usage: "zoom out on this code", "I don't understand this area", "how does this fit in the bigger picture"

Returns a map of relevant modules and callers using the project's domain glossary.

docs.title: ROI to human
docs.text: Get your bearings fast. No spelunking through 20 files to understand one module.

docs.title: Project Updates

docs.title: v0.1.0 — Repo init + skill setup
docs.text:
Date: 2026-05-19

Empty repo → tooled workspace:

  • Install juliusbrussee/caveman — ultra-compressed comms, ~75% token cut
  • Install mattpocock/skills — 14 engineering skills: diagnose, grill-me, tdd, triage, to-issues, to-prd, handoff, zoom-out, write-a-skill, prototype, improve-codebase-architecture, grill-with-docs, setup-matt-pocock-skills
  • Skills saved to .agents/skills/
  • AGENTS.md updated with full skill inventory

Next: define project purpose, create issues, build something.

docs.title: v0.2.0 — Docs site + CI/CD pipeline
docs.text:
Date: 2026-05-19

Docs infrastructure online:

  • mkdocs.yml — Material theme + Mermaid fences
  • docs/skills/ — demo files for all 14 skills
  • docs/index.md — entry page, links every skill demo
  • .github/workflows/docs.yml — 3-job workflow:
  • Push to main → build + deploy to gh-pages root
  • PR open/sync → build + deploy to gh-pages/preview/pr-#/
  • PR close → cleanup preview/pr-#/ folder
  • Docker image: squidfunk/mkdocs-material

Project updates folder (docs/updates/) live and tracked.

Next: set up issue tracker, define project goal, start building.

  • Install github/awesome-copilot@create-readme — 13.1K installs
  • README.md created: features, project structure, workflow table, quick start
  • docs/skills/create-readme-demo.md added

Next: define project purpose, start building.

docs.title: v0.4.0 — Projects section + style picker

Date: 2026-05-19

  • docs/projects/index.md — table of all mibracy GitHub repos
  • Style picker modal replaces reset button: pick primary/accent colors, persist to localStorage
